chunks.text: Self-introduction Self-introduction Appearance as of April 20, 2000. Biography I have been working for NEC Corporation in Japan since 1993. Now, I am conducting research about manipulation of micro electric parts at UC Berkeley for the company.

lectric parts at UC Berkeley for the company. Before joining the company, I studied in the Department of Electronic Engineering at Tohoku university in Japan and researched liquid crystal displays. I received the B.E. and M.E. degrees f

ays. I received the B.E. and M.E. degrees from the university, in 1991 and in 1993 respectively. At the company, I belong to Production Engineering Development Laboratory. I have been working on the development of production machi

n working on the development of production machines, such as bonding machines and servo track writers for hard disk drives. I used to design electrical circuits and software to drive and control these production machines. Publication in Eng

ol these production machines. Publication in English Control of polymer orientation in polymer dispersed liquid crystal (PDLC). E. Shimada and T. Uchida Japanese Journal of Applied Physics, Part 2 (Letters), vol.31, (no.3B), 15 March 1992. p.L352-4

Letters), vol.31, (no.3B), 15 March 1992. p.L352-4. Improvement of hysteresis in PDLC by control of polymer orientation. E. Shimada and T. Uchida Proceeding of the 12th International Display Research Conference, 12-14 October 1992. Hiroshima. Japan.
